answers.html: Small client-side preferences or saved interface state

questions.name: Persistence
questions.question_text: Data in localStorage usually remains after the browser tab is closed.

questions.name: sessionStorage
questions.question_text: sessionStorage differs from localStorage because it is usually scoped to:

questions.name: State model
questions.question_text: In UI development, state is best described as:

answers.text: The postal location of a user
The current data that determines what the interface shows
answers.html: The current data that determines what the interface shows

questions.name: Serialization
questions.question_text:
A developer wants to save the object below in localStorage:

const prefs = { theme: 'dark', compact: true }
localStorage.setItem('prefs', ???)

What should replace ????

questions.name: boolean-string-gotcha
questions.question_text: A developer stores localStorage.setItem('active', false) and later reads if (localStorage.getItem('active')). What happens?

answers.text: The condition is true, because localStorage stores the string 'false', and any non-empty string is truthy in JavaScript
answers.html: The condition is true, because localStorage stores the string 'false', and any non-empty string is truthy in JavaScript

answers.text: The condition is false, as expected, because false was stored
answers.html: The condition is false, as expected, because false was stored

answers.text: A TypeError is thrown because localStorage cannot store booleans
answers.html: A TypeError is thrown because localStorage cannot store booleans

answers.text: getItem returns null when the value is false
answers.html: getItem returns null when the value is false

questions.name: storage-persistence
questions.question_text: A user closes the browser tab and reopens the same site. Which storage still contains data they saved?

answers.text: localStorage — it persists until explicitly cleared, regardless of tab or browser restarts
answers.html: localStorage — it persists until explicitly cleared, regardless of tab or browser restarts

answers.text: sessionStorage — it persists across all sessions
Neither — all Web Storage is cleared when the tab closes
answers.html: Neither — all Web Storage is cleared when the tab closes

answers.text: Both — they have identical persistence behavior
